input.description:
swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.tsx:51-53

The computed field detection uses `(field.type as Record<string, unknown>).derive` which is a raw cast bypassing TypeScript's type system. If the FieldDef type discriminant shape ever changes, this silently breaks at runtime instead of failing at compile time.

Suggestion: Use a type guard or check the discriminant properly:
```ts
const isComputedTags =
  field.type.kind === "computed" &&
  "derive" in field.type &&
  field.type.derive === "parse-body-tags";
```
Or better, add a `derive` field to the computed variant in the TypeScript FieldType union if it doesn't already exist."
input.title: MultiSelectEditor uses unsafe type cast for computed field detection

output.description: The test at line 236 in ui/src/components/fields/editors/multi-select-editor.test.tsx wraps all assertions inside `if (view) { ... }` where `getCmView()` returns null in jsdom. The entire assertion block is skipped and the test passes vacuously with zero assertions. The `getCmView` helper looks for `cmEditor.cmView.view` on the DOM element, which is not populated by @uiw/react-codemirror in jsdom. The test needs to either: (1) find another way to dispatch text into CM6 in jsdom, or (2) use `expect(view).toBeTruthy()` and fix the underlying issue so the view is accessible.
output.title: False positive: "resolves typed text" test in multi-select-editor.test.tsx - getCmView returns null in jsdom

output.description: All 4 failures in src/lib/command-scope.test.tsx share the same root cause: `invoke("log_command", { cmd, target })` at command-scope.tsx:183 returns undefined in the test environment, so `.catch()` fails with TypeError. Tests affected: (1) executes a resolved command and returns true, (2) executes parent command when child does not register it, (3) handles async execute functions, (4) dispatchCommand > calls execute when set. Fix: mock `invoke` to return a Promise for the `log_command` call. #test-failure

input.description:
## What

Add `embedding_enabled: bool` to `IndexConfig` (default `true`). When `false`, `run_embedding_phase` skips entirely — no model load, no embedding, just parse + chunk + write to DB without embeddings.

This is the foundation for card 2 (making non-embedding tests fast) and card 3 (having a real embedding test that explicitly opts in).

### Files
- `swissarmyhammer-treesitter/src/index.rs`: Add field to `IndexConfig`, gate `run_embedding_phase`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `IndexConfig` has `embedding_enabled: bool`, default `true`
- [ ] When `embedding_enabled` is `false`, `scan_with_skip` completes without loading any model
- [ ] When `embedding_enabled` is `true`, behavior is unchanged from today
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (they all use default config which has `embedding_enabled: true`)

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test in `index.rs`: `test_config_embedding_disabled_skips_model` — create context with `embedding_enabled: false`, scan a dir with .rs files, assert `embedding_model` is `None` after scan completes and `files_embedded == 0`
- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-treesitter` — all existing tests pass
input.title: Add embedding_enabled flag to IndexConfig

output.description:
## What

Add `embedding_enabled: bool` to `IndexConfig` (default `true`). When `false`, `run_embedding_phase` skips entirely — no model load, no embedding, just parse + chunk + write to DB without embeddings.

This is the foundation for card 2 (making non-embedding tests fast) and card 3 (having a real embedding test that explicitly opts in).

### Files
- `swissarmyhammer-treesitter/src/index.rs`: Add field to `IndexConfig`, gate `run_embedding_phase`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `IndexConfig` has `embedding_enabled: bool`, default `true`
- [ ] When `embedding_enabled` is `false`, `scan_with_skip` completes without loading any model
- [ ] When `embedding_enabled` is `true`, behavior is unchanged from today
- [ ] Existing tests still pass (they all use default config which has `embedding_enabled: true`)

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test in `index.rs`: `test_config_embedding_disabled_skips_model` — create context with `embedding_enabled: false`, scan a dir with .rs files, assert `embedding_model` is `None` after scan completes and `files_embedded == 0`
- [ ] Run `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-treesitter` — all existing tests pass
output.title: Add embedding_enabled flag to IndexConfig

input.description:
## What

Replace the vacuous `test_leader_can_query_duplicates` / `test_find_all_duplicates_with_files` with a test that **actually proves** the embedding + duplicate detection pipeline works. The current tests assert `is_ok()` on empty results — they prove nothing.

### Key constraints from the code
- `cluster_by_similarity` only clusters chunks from **different files** (line 942 of unified.rs)
- Chunks are only created for `EMBEDDABLE_NODE_KINDS` — in Rust, that means `function_item`, `impl_item`, `struct_item`, etc.
- The test must create files with genuinely duplicate/near-duplicate function bodies across files
- `min_chunk_bytes` filters out tiny chunks, so functions need real bodies

### Test design
Create a temp workspace with 3 files:
- `utils_a.rs`: contains `fn process_data(items: &[i32]) -> Vec<i32> { items.iter().filter(|x| **x > 0).map(|x| x * 2).collect() }`
- `utils_b.rs`: contains a near-copy with trivial renames: `fn transform_data(values: &[i32]) -> Vec<i32> { values.iter().filter(|v| **v > 0).map(|v| v * 2).collect() }`
- `unrelated.rs`: contains a completely different function (string processing, not numeric)

Open workspace with `embedding_enabled: true`, wait for indexing, call `find_all_duplicates`. Assert:
1. At least one `DuplicateCluster` is returned
2. The cluster contains chunks from both `utils_a.rs` and `utils_b.rs`
3. `unrelated.rs` is NOT in the same cluster
4. The cluster's `avg_similarity` is above the threshold

### Files
- `swissarmyhammer-treesitter/src/unified.rs` (inline tests) or `tests/workspace_leader_reader.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Test creates workspace with duplicate code across files and a non-duplicate control file
- [ ] Test runs full indexing with embedding enabled
- [ ] Test asserts at least one duplicate cluster is found
- [ ] Test asserts the cluster contains the expected duplicate files and excludes the unrelated file
- [ ] Test asserts cluster similarity is above threshold

## Tests
- [ ] `test_find_all_duplicates_detects_near_identical_functions` — the test IS the deliverable
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-treesitter test_find_all_duplicates_detects` passes
input.title: Write a real end-to-end duplicate detection test

input.description:
## What

Now that `embedding_enabled` exists and we have a real embedding test, update all other workspace tests to use `embedding_enabled: false`. This means they only parse + write chunks to DB (no model download/load/inference), making the test suite fast.

### Scope
Two test locations need updating:
1. `swissarmyhammer-treesitter/src/unified.rs` inline tests — `open_and_wait()` helper and any test using `Workspace::new()` / `Workspace::open()` that doesn't need embeddings
2. `swissarmyhammer-treesitter/tests/workspace_leader_reader.rs` — same pattern

The `open_and_wait` helper should accept an optional `IndexConfig` or have a `open_and_wait_no_embedding` variant. Tests that need embeddings (the one from card 2) explicitly pass `embedding_enabled: true`.

### Approach
- Add `open_and_wait_with_config(dir, config)` helper
- Default `open_and_wait` uses `embedding_enabled: false`
- The real duplicate detection test uses `open_and_wait_with_config` with `embedding_enabled: true`
- Remove the vacuous duplicate tests that were replaced by card 2

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Non-embedding tests don't load any model
- [ ] Test suite runs significantly faster (seconds, not minutes)
- [ ] The real embedding test from card 2 still passes with embeddings enabled
- [ ] No test asserts `is_ok()` on duplicate results without checking actual content

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-treesitter` — all tests pass
- [ ] Timing: non-embedding tests complete in under 10 seconds total
input.title: Make non-embedding tests skip model loading

input.description:
## What
Change `ManagedDirectory::from_user_home()` in `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs` to resolve the user-level config root via XDG:
- `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/swissarmyhammer` (config: prompts, modes, skills, agents, commands, statusline)
- `$XDG_DATA_HOME/swissarmyhammer` (data: transcripts, questions, workflow-runs)
- Fall back to `~/.config/swissarmyhammer` and `~/.local/share/swissarmyhammer` per XDG spec
- Add a `DirectoryRootType::XdgConfig` and `DirectoryRootType::XdgData` variant
- Add `from_xdg_config()` and `from_xdg_data()` constructors
- Keep `from_user_home()` as a compat wrapper that delegates to `from_xdg_config()`

Also update the dot-directory resolver in `file_loader.rs` (`load_dot_directory_files` and `get_directories`) to use `dirs::config_dir()` instead of `dirs::home_dir()` for the User source.

Affected files:
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs`
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/file_loader.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ManagedDirectory::from_user_home()` resolves to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/<DIR_NAME>` when `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set
- [ ] Falls back to `~/.config/<DIR_NAME>` when `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is unset
- [ ] Dot-directory VFS mode uses XDG paths for User source
- [ ] Existing tests pass, new tests cover XDG env var override

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test: `from_user_home()` with `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` env var set → uses that path
- [ ] Unit test: `from_user_home()` without env var → uses `~/.config/`
- [ ] Unit test: dot-directory VFS loading uses config_dir()
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-directory`
input.title: Add XDG base directory resolution to ManagedDirectory::from_user_home()

input.description:
## What
Both `SkillResolver` and `AgentResolver` directly call `dirs::home_dir()` to construct user-level paths like `~/.skills`, `~/.swissarmyhammer/skills`, `~/.agents`, `~/.swissarmyhammer/agents`. They should use the VFS or `ManagedDirectory::from_user_home()` so XDG changes propagate automatically.

Affected files:
- `swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs` (lines 144, 264)
- `swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs` (line 108)

Approach: Create a helper in `swissarmyhammer-directory` that returns the user config root (XDG-aware), then use that in the resolvers instead of `dirs::home_dir()`. Or refactor to use `VirtualFileSystem` with appropriate search paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `skill_resolver.rs` no longer calls `dirs::home_dir()` directly
- [ ] `agent_resolver.rs` no longer calls `dirs::home_dir()` directly
- [ ] Skills and agents still load from user-level directories
- [ ] User-level paths now resolve via XDG

## Tests
- [ ] Existing skill/agent loading tests still pass
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-skills -p swissarmyhammer-agents`
input.title: Route skill_resolver and agent_resolver through VFS instead of dirs::home_dir()

input.description:
## What
Several other modules bypass the VFS and call `dirs::home_dir()` directly. Each needs to be routed through the XDG-aware path resolution.

Bypass sites:
1. **`swissarmyhammer-config/src/model.rs:1395`** — `load_user_models()` uses `home.join(".models")`. Should use XDG config dir.
2. **`swissarmyhammer-statusline/src/config.rs:437`** — `load_config()` uses `home.join(".swissarmyhammer").join("statusline")`. Should use `ManagedDirectory::from_user_home().subdir("statusline")`.
3. **`swissarmyhammer-tools/src/health_registry.rs:44,81`** — health checks use `home.join(".prompts")`. Should use XDG config dir or VFS get_directories().
4. **`swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/src/state.rs:315`** — `auto_open_board` home backstop. This one is semantically different (searching for `.kanban` in home) — may need `dirs::home_dir()` retained or use a kanban-specific XDG path.
5. **`swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/prompt/new.rs:27`** — prompt creation in home dir.

Approach: Add a `user_config_root()` free function to `swissarmyhammer-directory` that returns the XDG config home. Replace all `dirs::home_dir()` calls with this function + appropriate subpath.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No remaining `dirs::home_dir()` calls for config/data paths (grep confirms)
- [ ] All user-level paths resolve through XDG
- [ ] Kanban home backstop uses appropriate resolution
- [ ] All existing tests pass

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo test` full workspace passes
- [ ] Manual verification: `grep -r 'dirs::home_dir' --include='*.rs'` shows only the new XDG helper or justified exceptions
input.title: Route remaining dirs::home_dir() callers through XDG-aware helpers

input.description:
## What
Users with existing `~/.swissarmyhammer`, `~/.prompts`, `~/.skills`, `~/.agents`, `~/.models` directories need a migration path. On first run with the new code, if the XDG location doesn't exist but the legacy `~/.<name>` does, either:
- Option A: Automatically symlink or copy
- Option B: Log a warning with migration instructions
- Option C: Check both locations with legacy as fallback (read from old, write to new)

This card decides the strategy and implements it in `ManagedDirectory::from_user_home()` or a new `migrate_legacy_home()` helper.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Existing users with `~/.swissarmyhammer` continue to work without manual intervention
- [ ] New users get XDG paths by default
- [ ] Migration strategy is documented in code comments
- [ ] No data loss scenario exists

## Tests
- [ ] Test: legacy path exists, XDG path doesn't → legacy path used (or migrated)
- [ ] Test: XDG path exists → XDG path used regardless of legacy
- [ ] Test: neither exists → XDG path created
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-directory`
input.title: Add migration support for existing ~/.swissarmyhammer to XDG location

Both `SkillResolver` and `AgentResolver` directly call `dirs::home_dir()` to construct user-level paths like `~/.skills`, `~/.swissarmyhammer/skills`, `~/.agents`, `~/.swissarmyhammer/agents`. They should use the VFS or `ManagedDirectory::from_user_home()` so XDG changes propagate automatically.

Affected files:
- `swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs` (lines 144, 264)
- `swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs` (line 108)

Approach: Create a helper in `swissarmyhammer-directory` that returns the user config root (XDG-aware), then use that in the resolvers instead of `dirs::home_dir()`. Or refactor to use `VirtualFileSystem` with appropriate search paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `skill_resolver.rs` no longer calls `dirs::home_dir()` directly
- [ ] `agent_resolver.rs` no longer calls `dirs::home_dir()` directly
- [ ] Skills and agents still load from user-level directories
- [ ] User-level paths now resolve via XDG

## Tests
- [ ] Existing skill/agent loading tests still pass
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-skills -p swissarmyhammer-agents`
output.title: Replace dirs::home_dir() in agent_resolver.rs load_from_user_paths()

input.title: Implement legacy fallback: check ~/.<name> when XDG path doesn't exist

output.description:
## What
Users with existing `~/.swissarmyhammer`, `~/.prompts`, `~/.skills`, `~/.agents`, `~/.models` directories need a migration path. On first run with the new code, if the XDG location doesn't exist but the legacy `~/.<name>` does, either:
- Option A: Automatically symlink or copy
- Option B: Log a warning with migration instructions
- Option C: Check both locations with legacy as fallback (read from old, write to new)

This card decides the strategy and implements it in `ManagedDirectory::from_user_home()` or a new `migrate_legacy_home()` helper.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Existing users with `~/.swissarmyhammer` continue to work without manual intervention
- [ ] New users get XDG paths by default
- [ ] Migration strategy is documented in code comments
- [ ] No data loss scenario exists

## Tests
- [ ] Test: legacy path exists, XDG path doesn't → legacy path used (or migrated)
- [ ] Test: XDG path exists → XDG path used regardless of legacy
- [ ] Test: neither exists → XDG path created
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-directory`
output.title: Implement legacy fallback: check ~/.<name> when XDG path doesn't exist

input.description:
## What
Change `SwissarmyhammerConfig::DIR_NAME` from `".swissarmyhammer"` to `".sah"` in `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/config.rs`. This is the canonical "one place" for the directory name.

However, 16 hardcoded `".swissarmyhammer"` string literals across 8 files bypass `DIR_NAME` and must be replaced with references to the constant. These are in:

1. **`swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`** (2) — `cwd.join(".swissarmyhammer").join("agents")`, `home.join(".swissarmyhammer").join("agents")`
2. **`swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs`** (4) — same pattern for skills
3. **`swissarmyhammer-config/src/model.rs`** (2) — config path joining
4. **`swissarmyhammer-common/src/glob_utils.rs`** (1) — hidden dir exclusion list
5. **`swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/install/components/mod.rs`** (1) — `cwd.join(".swissarmyhammer")`
6. **`swissarmyhammer-statusline/src/config.rs`** (2) — user/project config paths
7. **`swissarmyhammer-directory/src/config.rs`** (3) — the DIR_NAME itself + tests
8. **`swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs`** (1) — test assertion

Approach: Replace all hardcoded `".swissarmyhammer"` with `SwissarmyhammerConfig::DIR_NAME` (or a re-exported `SAH_DIR_NAME` const for convenience). Then change the single constant to `".sah"`.

Also update ~60 comment/doc references from `.swissarmyhammer` to `.sah` for consistency.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `SwissarmyhammerConfig::DIR_NAME` is `".sah"`
- [ ] `grep -r '"\. swissarmyhammer"' --include='*.rs'` returns zero hits (no hardcoded literals)
- [ ] All code paths use `DIR_NAME` or `ManagedDirectory::dir_name()`
- [ ] Comments/docs updated to reference `.sah`
- [ ] All tests pass

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo test` full workspace
- [ ] Verify directory name in test assertions uses the constant

input.description:
## What
Users with existing directories need a migration path for TWO changes:
1. **XDG move**: `~/.swissarmyhammer` → `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/sah` (or `~/.config/sah`)
2. **Rename**: `.swissarmyhammer` → `.sah` (both at ~ level and project level)

Also covers legacy dot-directories: `~/.prompts`, `~/.skills`, `~/.agents`, `~/.models`

Strategy: Option C — check both locations with legacy as fallback (read from old, write to new). On first run, if XDG path doesn't exist but legacy does, use legacy + log deprecation warning.

Affected files:
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs` — `from_user_home()` legacy check
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/file_loader.rs` — dot-directory legacy check

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Existing users with `~/.swissarmyhammer` continue to work
- [ ] Existing users with project-level `.swissarmyhammer/` continue to work
- [ ] New users get `.sah` at XDG paths by default
- [ ] Deprecation warning logged when legacy path is used
- [ ] No data loss scenario exists

## Tests
- [ ] Test: legacy `~/.swissarmyhammer` exists, `.sah` doesn't → legacy used
- [ ] Test: `.sah` exists → `.sah` used regardless of legacy
- [ ] Test: neither exists → `.sah` created at XDG path
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-directory`

input.description:
## What
There are 16 sites that manually construct paths like `cwd.join(".swissarmyhammer").join("skills")`. The fix is NOT to replace the string with `DIR_NAME` — it's to route these callers through `ManagedDirectory` which already handles path resolution.

Replace patterns like:
```rust
cwd.join(".swissarmyhammer").join("agents")
home.join(".swissarmyhammer").join("skills")
```
With:
```rust
ManagedDirectory::<SwissarmyhammerConfig>::from_git_root()?.subdir("agents")
```

output.description:
## What
Change `ManagedDirectory::from_user_home()` in `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs` to resolve the user-level config root via XDG:
- `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/swissarmyhammer` (config: prompts, modes, skills, agents, commands, statusline)
- `$XDG_DATA_HOME/swissarmyhammer` (data: transcripts, questions, workflow-runs)
- Fall back to `~/.config/swissarmyhammer` and `~/.local/share/swissarmyhammer` per XDG spec
- Add a `DirectoryRootType::XdgConfig` and `DirectoryRootType::XdgData` variant
- Add `from_xdg_config()` and `from_xdg_data()` constructors
- Keep `from_user_home()` as a compat wrapper that delegates to `from_xdg_config()`

Also update the dot-directory resolver in `file_loader.rs` (`load_dot_directory_files` and `get_directories`) to use `dirs::config_dir()` instead of `dirs::home_dir()` for the User source.

Affected files:
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs`
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/file_loader.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ManagedDirectory::from_user_home()` resolves to `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/<DIR_NAME>` when `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is set
- [ ] Falls back to `~/.config/<DIR_NAME>` when `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` is unset
- [ ] Dot-directory VFS mode uses XDG paths for User source
- [ ] Existing tests pass, new tests cover XDG env var override

## Tests
- [ ] Unit test: `from_user_home()` with `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME` env var set → uses that path
- [ ] Unit test: `from_user_home()` without env var → uses `~/.config/`
- [ ] Unit test: dot-directory VFS loading uses config_dir()
- [ ] `cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-directory`
output.title: Add unit tests for XDG env var override and default fallback

input.description:
swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs:280

`try_promote()` has a comment reading "Open (not create) — the file already exists from the original election" but the implementation calls `File::create(&self.lock_path)`. `File::create` truncates-or-creates. If the file was removed (e.g. by the outgoing leader's Drop impl, which now deletes the lock file), a follower calling `try_promote()` would silently create a fresh 0-byte lock file and immediately win the lock — even with no prior leader. This is a correctness issue: the comment and the code disagree, and there is a real race between `LeaderGuard::drop` removing the lock file and a follower calling `File::create` to re-create it.

Suggestion: decide on one of two approaches:
1. Don't delete the lock file in `LeaderGuard::drop` — keep it as a stable sentinel file, and both `try_acquire_lock` and `try_promote` open it with `File::create` (current create-or-truncate semantics are fine).
2. Use `OpenOptions::new().write(true).create(false).open(...)` in `try_promote` and return `Ok(None)` on `NotFound` — treat a missing lock file the same as a free lock. Fix the comment to match.

Currently option 1 is effectively what the code does, so the comment should be removed/corrected.", 
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"]
input.title: FollowerGuard: comment says "Open (not create)" but uses File::create

output.description:

This is a correctness issue: the comment and the code disagree, and there is a real race between `LeaderGuard::drop` removing the lock file and a follower calling `File::create` to re-create it.

Suggestion: decide on one of two approaches:
1. Don't delete the lock file in `LeaderGuard::drop` — keep it as a stable sentinel file, and both `try_acquire_lock` and `try_promote` open it with `File::create` (current create-or-truncate semantics are fine).
2. Use `OpenOptions::new().write(true).create(false).open(...)` in `try_promote` and return `Ok(None)` on `NotFound` — treat a missing lock file the same as a free lock. Fix the comment to match.

Currently option 1 is effectively what the code does, so the comment should be removed/corrected.",

input.description:
swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs:406-444

When a follower is promoted via the re-election loop, `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` is called, which starts TS indexing, file watcher, AND attempts to start LSP workers by checking `LSP_SUPERVISOR.get()`. However, the LSP supervisor startup (`lsp_handle.await`) runs in a *separate* tokio task that awaits the LSP handle before starting LSP workers for an already-leader workspace. If a follower is promoted AFTER the LSP health-check task has already awaited `lsp_handle` and moved into the periodic health-check loop, the promotion path's LSP worker code in `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` may succeed. But if the LSP supervisor has not yet completed startup when promotion fires, `LSP_SUPERVISOR.get()` returns `None` and LSP indexing is silently skipped with no retry.

This is a warning: a process that wins leadership after the original leader dies during LSP startup will index with TS only, never with LSP, for the lifetime of the process.

Suggestion: Have `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` either (a) wait for `LSP_SUPERVISOR` to be populated with a short poll/notify before starting LSP workers, or (b) document clearly that LSP workers will not run for a promoted follower if LSP hasn't finished starting yet, and log a warning when `LSP_SUPERVISOR.get()` returns `None`.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"]
input.title: Re-election loop: LSP workers not started on promotion

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs:406-444

When a follower is promoted via the re-election loop, `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` is called, which starts TS indexing, file watcher, AND attempts to start LSP workers by checking `LSP_SUPERVISOR.get()`. However, the LSP supervisor startup (`lsp_handle.await`) runs in a *separate* tokio task that awaits the LSP handle before starting LSP workers for an already-leader workspace. If a follower is promoted AFTER the LSP health-check task has already awaited `lsp_handle` and moved into the periodic health-check loop, the promotion path's LSP worker code in `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` may succeed. But if the LSP supervisor has not yet completed startup when promotion fires, `LSP_SUPERVISOR.get()` returns `None` and LSP indexing is silently skipped with no retry.

This is a warning: a process that wins leadership after the original leader dies during LSP startup will index with TS only, never with LSP, for the lifetime of the process.

Suggestion: Have `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` either (a) wait for `LSP_SUPERVISOR` to be populated with a short poll/notify before starting LSP workers, or (b) document clearly that LSP workers will not run for a promoted follower if LSP hasn't finished starting yet, and log a warning when `LSP_SUPERVISOR.get()` returns `None`.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"]
output.title: Re-election loop: LSP workers not started on promotion

input.description:
swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs:411-444

The re-election loop spawns a `tokio::spawn` that runs `loop { sleep(5s); ... }` indefinitely. Once a follower is promoted to leader, the loop body hits the `if ws_lock.is_leader() { continue; }` branch every 5 seconds for the rest of the process lifetime. This is minor overhead but wastes a mutex lock + mode check every 5 seconds forever.

More importantly, there is no shutdown/cancellation mechanism for either the re-election loop or the LSP health-check loop. When the server shuts down, these tasks are just abandoned. For a library crate this is at minimum a leak of tokio task handles with no way to join/cancel them.

Suggestion: Use a `tokio::sync::watch` channel or a `CancellationToken` (from `tokio-util`) so both background loops can be stopped cleanly. Or at minimum, break out of the re-election loop once promotion succeeds — no further re-elections are needed.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"]
input.title: Re-election loop runs forever even after promotion — wasted CPU for leaders

output.description:

swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs:68, 235, 265

The three new/modified public types `ElectionOutcome`, `LeaderGuard`, and `FollowerGuard` have no `#[derive(Debug)]`. Per the Rust review guidelines, all public types must implement `Debug`. `ElectionConfig` correctly derives `Debug` and `Clone`; the other types do not.

`LeaderGuard` cannot derive `Debug` automatically (it contains `File`), but a manual impl is trivial: output "LeaderGuard" as the struct name. `FollowerGuard` can derive it. `ElectionOutcome` can derive it if both variants implement `Debug`.

Suggestion: Add `#[derive(Debug)]` to `FollowerGuard`. Add manual `impl Debug for LeaderGuard` and `impl Debug for ElectionOutcome`.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"]
input.title: ElectionOutcome, LeaderGuard, FollowerGuard missing Debug derive

.config/nextest.toml:38

The `treesitter-embedding` test group is applied via:
```toml
filter = "test(test_find_all_duplicates_detects_near_identical_functions)"
```
This hardcodes a single test function name. If future embedding-heavy tests are added to the treesitter crate with different names, they will not pick up the serialisation constraint. The llama-embedding group correctly uses a package filter (`package(llama-embedding)`) which is robust to test name changes.

Suggestion: Use a package-scoped filter instead of a bare test name:
```toml
filter = "package(swissarmyhammer-treesitter) and test(embedding)"
```
or a more targeted package filter if the entire package should be serialised. At minimum document why a name-based filter is intentional here.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"]
input.title: nextest.toml treesitter-embedding group: filter too narrow (single test name)

swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs:280

`try_promote()` has a comment reading "Open (not create) — the file already exists from the original election" but the implementation calls `File::create(&self.lock_path)`. `File::create` truncates-or-creates. If the file was removed (e.g. by the outgoing leader's Drop impl, which now deletes the lock file), a follower calling `try_promote()` would silently create a fresh 0-byte lock file and immediately win the lock — even with no prior leader. This is a correctness issue: the comment and the code disagree, and there is a real race between `LeaderGuard::drop` removing the lock file and a follower calling `File::create` to re-create it.

Suggestion: decide on one of two approaches:
1. Don't delete the lock file in `LeaderGuard::drop` — keep it as a stable sentinel file, and both `try_acquire_lock` and `try_promote` open it with `File::create` (current create-or-truncate semantics are fine).
2. Use `OpenOptions::new().write(true).create(false).open(...)` in `try_promote` and return `Ok(None)` on `NotFound` — treat a missing lock file the same as a free lock. Fix the comment to match.

Currently option 1 is effectively what the code does, so the comment should be removed/corrected.", 
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"] #review-finding
output.title: FollowerGuard: comment says "Open (not create)" but uses File::create

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs:406-444

When a follower is promoted via the re-election loop, `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` is called, which starts TS indexing, file watcher, AND attempts to start LSP workers by checking `LSP_SUPERVISOR.get()`. However, the LSP supervisor startup (`lsp_handle.await`) runs in a *separate* tokio task that awaits the LSP handle before starting LSP workers for an already-leader workspace. If a follower is promoted AFTER the LSP health-check task has already awaited `lsp_handle` and moved into the periodic health-check loop, the promotion path's LSP worker code in `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` may succeed. But if the LSP supervisor has not yet completed startup when promotion fires, `LSP_SUPERVISOR.get()` returns `None` and LSP indexing is silently skipped with no retry.

This is a warning: a process that wins leadership after the original leader dies during LSP startup will index with TS only, never with LSP, for the lifetime of the process.

Suggestion: Have `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` either (a) wait for `LSP_SUPERVISOR` to be populated with a short poll/notify before starting LSP workers, or (b) document clearly that LSP workers will not run for a promoted follower if LSP hasn't finished starting yet, and log a warning when `LSP_SUPERVISOR.get()` returns `None`.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"] #review-finding
output.title: Re-election loop: LSP workers not started on promotion

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs:411-444

The re-election loop spawns a `tokio::spawn` that runs `loop { sleep(5s); ... }` indefinitely. Once a follower is promoted to leader, the loop body hits the `if ws_lock.is_leader() { continue; }` branch every 5 seconds for the rest of the process lifetime. This is minor overhead but wastes a mutex lock + mode check every 5 seconds forever.

More importantly, there is no shutdown/cancellation mechanism for either the re-election loop or the LSP health-check loop. When the server shuts down, these tasks are just abandoned. For a library crate this is at minimum a leak of tokio task handles with no way to join/cancel them.

Suggestion: Use a `tokio::sync::watch` channel or a `CancellationToken` (from `tokio-util`) so both background loops can be stopped cleanly. Or at minimum, break out of the re-election loop once promotion succeeds — no further re-elections are needed.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"] #review-finding
output.title: Re-election loop runs forever even after promotion — wasted CPU for leaders

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs:68, 235, 265

The three new/modified public types `ElectionOutcome`, `LeaderGuard`, and `FollowerGuard` have no `#[derive(Debug)]`. Per the Rust review guidelines, all public types must implement `Debug`. `ElectionConfig` correctly derives `Debug` and `Clone`; the other types do not.

`LeaderGuard` cannot derive `Debug` automatically (it contains `File`), but a manual impl is trivial: output "LeaderGuard" as the struct name. `FollowerGuard` can derive it. `ElectionOutcome` can derive it if both variants implement `Debug`.

Suggestion: Add `#[derive(Debug)]` to `FollowerGuard`. Add manual `impl Debug for LeaderGuard` and `impl Debug for ElectionOutcome`.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"] #review-finding
output.title: ElectionOutcome, LeaderGuard, FollowerGuard missing Debug derive

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/lib.rs:17-38

The crate-level doc comment still shows the old `try_become_leader()` / `Err(ElectionError::LockHeld)` pattern as the primary example. The code comment on `elect()` says it is the preferred entry point for new code, but the crate docs were not updated. A new user of the crate will see the legacy API first.

Suggestion: Update the crate-level example to use `elect()` and `ElectionOutcome::Leader`/`Follower` matching. The old example can remain as a secondary "legacy" example or be removed.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"] #review-finding
output.title: lib.rs crate docs still show old try_become_leader() API in example

output.description:
swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/workspace.rs:59

The `CodeContextWorkspace` struct has a field comment `/// The mode (leader or reader)` which was not updated when `WorkspaceMode::Reader` was renamed to `WorkspaceMode::Follower`. This is a stale comment that will confuse readers.

Suggestion: Change to `/// The mode (leader or follower)`.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"] #review-finding
output.title: CodeContextWorkspace struct doc comment refers to \"reader\" not \"follower\"

output.description:
.config/nextest.toml:38

The `treesitter-embedding` test group is applied via:
```toml
filter = "test(test_find_all_duplicates_detects_near_identical_functions)"
```
This hardcodes a single test function name. If future embedding-heavy tests are added to the treesitter crate with different names, they will not pick up the serialisation constraint. The llama-embedding group correctly uses a package filter (`package(llama-embedding)`) which is robust to test name changes.

Suggestion: Use a package-scoped filter instead of a bare test name:
```toml
filter = "package(swissarmyhammer-treesitter) and test(embedding)"
```
or a more targeted package filter if the entire package should be serialised. At minimum document why a name-based filter is intentional here.",
<parameter name="tags">["review-finding"] #review-finding
output.title: nextest.toml treesitter-embedding group: filter too narrow (single test name)

input.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs`
**Severity:** blocker

`start_indexing_workers` (lines ~495–523) and `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` (lines ~527–624) share identical tree-sitter and file-watcher spawn logic. The only difference is that the promotion variant adds a third task that polls for the LSP supervisor. This violates DRY in a meaningful way: any bug fix or new worker added to one copy must be manually mirrored to the other.

**Fix:** Extract a shared `spawn_ts_and_watcher_workers(workspace_root, shared_db)` helper called by both, then add the LSP polling task only in the promotion path."
input.title: [blocker] `start_indexing_workers` and `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` are near-identical — duplicated code

input.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs` line 142
**Severity:** blocker

`hash_path` uses MD5 and truncates to 12 hex characters (48 bits). MD5 is cryptographically broken and, more practically, the 12-character truncation means only 48 bits of the digest are used. Two different workspace paths that collide in those 48 bits would share a lock file, silently serialising unrelated processes or preventing either from becoming leader.

MD5 is also not in the workspace's standard dependency set — it was likely pulled in just for this. The standard library's `DefaultHasher` is not stable across processes/versions, but `std::hash` + `seahash` or `xxhash` (already common in Rust ecosystems) would be better choices, or simply hex-encode a longer SHA-256 prefix via `sha2`.

**Fix:** Replace with a non-cryptographic hash with better avalanche properties and use at least 16 hex chars, or use the full 32-char MD5 hex digest instead of truncating."
input.title: [blocker] `LeaderElection` uses MD5 for path hashing — collision risk for lock-file uniqueness

input.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` lines ~297–491
**Severity:** warning

`initialize_code_context` is declared `fn` (not `async fn`) but it calls `tokio::spawn` three times internally. This is only sound if the caller guarantees a Tokio runtime is active. If ever called from a non-async context (e.g. a test or a future refactor) it will panic with "no reactor running".

The `std::sync::Once` guard is used to protect the spawn, which is fine, but the signature hides the Tokio dependency. A doc comment stating the requirement, or making it `async fn` (and using `tokio::spawn` from within), would make the constraint explicit.

**Fix:** Add a `# Panics` doc comment: "Panics if called outside a Tokio runtime." Alternatively restructure as `async fn` and remove the Once (callers can do their own deduplication with a `OnceCell<JoinHandle>`)."
input.title: [warning] `initialize_code_context` is a sync fn that calls `tokio::spawn` — must be called from within a Tokio runtime

input.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/workspace.rs` line 160
**Severity:** warning

```rust
DbRef::Shared(db.lock().unwrap_or_else(|p| p.into_inner()))
```

If the mutex is poisoned (because a writer panicked mid-operation), this recovers the poisoned guard and hands the caller a potentially-corrupt database connection. The correct action is to propagate the poison as an error so the caller can decide whether to abandon the workspace. The same pattern appears in `do_initialize_code_context` (server.rs lines 400, 418) where `ws.lock().unwrap_or_else(|p| p.into_inner())` silently recovers.

**Fix:** Change `db()` to return `Result<DbRef<'_>, CodeContextError>` and propagate the `PoisonError` as a new `CodeContextError::Poisoned` variant, or at minimum call `.expect("workspace db mutex poisoned")` so the process fails loudly rather than continuing with bad state."
input.title: [warning] `CodeContextWorkspace::db()` calls `unwrap_or_else(|p| p.into_inner())` on a poisoned mutex — silently ignores panic in a write thread

input.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/error.rs` line 22
**Severity:** warning

`ElectionError::SocketError(io::Error)` appears in the error enum and is tested in `test_all_error_variants_display`, but there is no site in `election.rs` that ever constructs it. Socket file removal uses `let _ = fs::remove_file(...)` (errors silently discarded). This is dead code in the public API of a library crate.

Either:
1. Remove the variant if socket errors are intentionally swallowed (the current design), or
2. Actually propagate socket creation/removal errors through it.

A dead public error variant is a semver hazard and misleads callers about what errors they should handle."
input.title: [warning] `ElectionError::SocketError` variant is defined but never constructed

input.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/workspace.rs`
**Severity:** warning

`WorkspaceMode` has a manual `Debug` impl that produces just `"Leader"` or `"Follower"` — the workspace root and mode details are lost. `DbRef` has no `Debug` impl at all. Per the Rust review guidelines, all public types must implement `Debug`.

For `DbRef`, a minimal impl like `f.debug_struct("DbRef").finish_non_exhaustive()` would suffice since `Connection` is not `Debug` either. For `WorkspaceMode`, the existing impl is acceptable but should include the mode label at minimum (which it does), though adding the workspace root would aid diagnostics.

**Fix:** Add `impl fmt::Debug for DbRef<'_>` with a field-less struct form."

input.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/workspace.rs` lines 131–135
**Severity:** warning

When `elect()` returns `Follower`, the code immediately attempts to open the database read-only:

```rust
let db = Connection::open_with_flags(&db_path, SQLITE_OPEN_READ_ONLY | ...)?;
```

But if this is the very first run, the leader hasn't had a chance to create `index.db` yet (it does so on the same path, moments later in its own branch). The follower's `open_with_flags` call will fail with "unable to open database file" because SQLite read-only open does not create the file.

The test `test_second_open_is_reader` masks this because both opens happen in the same process where the leader branch runs first. In a real multi-process scenario with tight timing the follower will get an `Err`.

**Fix:** Retry the follower DB open with a short backoff (or wait for the DB file to appear), or have the leader create the DB file before returning from `elect()` via a pre-creation step."
input.title: [warning] Follower workspace opens DB before the leader has created it — races on startup

input.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` lines ~412–446
**Severity:** warning

The re-election loop breaks as soon as promotion succeeds or when `ws_lock.is_leader()` is true. Once exited it never re-runs. This means:

1. If the promoted leader's `LeaderGuard` is later dropped for any reason (e.g. another call to `try_promote` on the same workspace inadvertently drops the guard), the process silently becomes leaderless with no recovery.
2. If `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` panics and the spawned task aborts, the workspace still holds the leader lock but no workers are running — there is no watchdog.

The loop is also polling every 5 seconds unconditionally. A `tokio::sync::Notify` or `watch` channel from `LeaderGuard::dropped` would be cleaner.

**Fix:** At minimum, document that the loop is one-shot (only promotes once). For production resilience, keep the loop alive to handle unexpected demotion."
input.title: [warning] Re-election loop in `do_initialize_code_context` exits on first promotion but never handles leader-to-follower demotion

input.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs` line 60
**Severity:** nit

```rust
fn base_dir(&self) -> PathBuf {
    self.base_dir.clone().unwrap_or_else(std::env::temp_dir)
}
```

This always clones the `Option<PathBuf>` even in the `Some` branch. Prefer:

```rust
fn base_dir(&self) -> PathBuf {
    self.base_dir.as_deref()
        .map(PathBuf::from)
        .unwrap_or_else(std::env::temp_dir)
}
```

Or simply keep the clone but acknowledge it's a minor allocation on the hot path of initialisation (not in a loop). Low impact, but the pattern teaches the wrong idiom."
input.title: [nit] `ElectionConfig::base_dir()` (private getter) clones `PathBuf` unnecessarily

input.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-treesitter/tests/workspace_leader_reader.rs`
**Severity:** nit

The Rust review guidelines state "Examples use `?`, not `.unwrap()`." While tests are not documentation examples, consistent use of `unwrap()` without any message makes test failure output unhelpful ("called `Option::unwrap()` on a `None` value" with no context). The file uses `.unwrap()` on ~20 call sites.

For tests, prefer `.expect("descriptive message")` at every `unwrap()` call so failures identify *what* was expected. For example:
```rust
let workspace = Workspace::new(dir).open().await.expect("workspace should open");
```

The assertion messages in `assert!` and `assert_eq!` are already good — just the unwrap sites need messages."
input.title: [nit] Treesitter integration tests use `unwrap()` throughout — contradicts doc guidelines

`start_indexing_workers` (lines ~495–523) and `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` (lines ~527–624) share identical tree-sitter and file-watcher spawn logic. The only difference is that the promotion variant adds a third task that polls for the LSP supervisor. This violates DRY in a meaningful way: any bug fix or new worker added to one copy must be manually mirrored to the other.

**Fix:** Extract a shared `spawn_ts_and_watcher_workers(workspace_root, shared_db)` helper called by both, then add the LSP polling task only in the promotion path." #review-finding

output.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs` line 142
**Severity:** blocker

`hash_path` uses MD5 and truncates to 12 hex characters (48 bits). MD5 is cryptographically broken and, more practically, the 12-character truncation means only 48 bits of the digest are used. Two different workspace paths that collide in those 48 bits would share a lock file, silently serialising unrelated processes or preventing either from becoming leader.

MD5 is also not in the workspace's standard dependency set — it was likely pulled in just for this. The standard library's `DefaultHasher` is not stable across processes/versions, but `std::hash` + `seahash` or `xxhash` (already common in Rust ecosystems) would be better choices, or simply hex-encode a longer SHA-256 prefix via `sha2`.

**Fix:** Replace with a non-cryptographic hash with better avalanche properties and use at least 16 hex chars, or use the full 32-char MD5 hex digest instead of truncating." #review-finding
output.title: [blocker] `LeaderElection` uses MD5 for path hashing — collision risk for lock-file uniqueness

output.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` lines ~297–491
**Severity:** warning

`initialize_code_context` is declared `fn` (not `async fn`) but it calls `tokio::spawn` three times internally. This is only sound if the caller guarantees a Tokio runtime is active. If ever called from a non-async context (e.g. a test or a future refactor) it will panic with "no reactor running".

The `std::sync::Once` guard is used to protect the spawn, which is fine, but the signature hides the Tokio dependency. A doc comment stating the requirement, or making it `async fn` (and using `tokio::spawn` from within), would make the constraint explicit.

**Fix:** Add a `# Panics` doc comment: "Panics if called outside a Tokio runtime." Alternatively restructure as `async fn` and remove the Once (callers can do their own deduplication with a `OnceCell<JoinHandle>`)." #review-finding
output.title: [warning] `initialize_code_context` is a sync fn that calls `tokio::spawn` — must be called from within a Tokio runtime

output.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/workspace.rs` line 160
**Severity:** warning

```rust
DbRef::Shared(db.lock().unwrap_or_else(|p| p.into_inner()))
```

If the mutex is poisoned (because a writer panicked mid-operation), this recovers the poisoned guard and hands the caller a potentially-corrupt database connection. The correct action is to propagate the poison as an error so the caller can decide whether to abandon the workspace. The same pattern appears in `do_initialize_code_context` (server.rs lines 400, 418) where `ws.lock().unwrap_or_else(|p| p.into_inner())` silently recovers.

**Fix:** Change `db()` to return `Result<DbRef<'_>, CodeContextError>` and propagate the `PoisonError` as a new `CodeContextError::Poisoned` variant, or at minimum call `.expect("workspace db mutex poisoned")` so the process fails loudly rather than continuing with bad state." #review-finding
output.title: [warning] `CodeContextWorkspace::db()` calls `unwrap_or_else(|p| p.into_inner())` on a poisoned mutex — silently ignores panic in a write thread

**File:** `swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/error.rs` line 22
**Severity:** warning

`ElectionError::SocketError(io::Error)` appears in the error enum and is tested in `test_all_error_variants_display`, but there is no site in `election.rs` that ever constructs it. Socket file removal uses `let _ = fs::remove_file(...)` (errors silently discarded). This is dead code in the public API of a library crate.

Either:
1. Remove the variant if socket errors are intentionally swallowed (the current design), or
2. Actually propagate socket creation/removal errors through it.

A dead public error variant is a semver hazard and misleads callers about what errors they should handle." #review-finding
output.title: [warning] `ElectionError::SocketError` variant is defined but never constructed

**File:** `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/workspace.rs`
**Severity:** warning

`WorkspaceMode` has a manual `Debug` impl that produces just `"Leader"` or `"Follower"` — the workspace root and mode details are lost. `DbRef` has no `Debug` impl at all. Per the Rust review guidelines, all public types must implement `Debug`.

For `DbRef`, a minimal impl like `f.debug_struct("DbRef").finish_non_exhaustive()` would suffice since `Connection` is not `Debug` either. For `WorkspaceMode`, the existing impl is acceptable but should include the mode label at minimum (which it does), though adding the workspace root would aid diagnostics.

**Fix:** Add `impl fmt::Debug for DbRef<'_>` with a field-less struct form." #review-finding
output.title: [warning] `WorkspaceMode` and `DbRef` are missing `Debug` for `Follower.db` and `DbRef` variants

output.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-code-context/src/workspace.rs` lines 131–135
**Severity:** warning

When `elect()` returns `Follower`, the code immediately attempts to open the database read-only:

```rust
let db = Connection::open_with_flags(&db_path, SQLITE_OPEN_READ_ONLY | ...)?;
```

But if this is the very first run, the leader hasn't had a chance to create `index.db` yet (it does so on the same path, moments later in its own branch). The follower's `open_with_flags` call will fail with "unable to open database file" because SQLite read-only open does not create the file.

The test `test_second_open_is_reader` masks this because both opens happen in the same process where the leader branch runs first. In a real multi-process scenario with tight timing the follower will get an `Err`.

**Fix:** Retry the follower DB open with a short backoff (or wait for the DB file to appear), or have the leader create the DB file before returning from `elect()` via a pre-creation step." #review-finding
output.title: [warning] Follower workspace opens DB before the leader has created it — races on startup

output.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/server.rs` lines ~412–446
**Severity:** warning

The re-election loop breaks as soon as promotion succeeds or when `ws_lock.is_leader()` is true. Once exited it never re-runs. This means:

1. If the promoted leader's `LeaderGuard` is later dropped for any reason (e.g. another call to `try_promote` on the same workspace inadvertently drops the guard), the process silently becomes leaderless with no recovery.
2. If `start_indexing_workers_after_promotion` panics and the spawned task aborts, the workspace still holds the leader lock but no workers are running — there is no watchdog.

The loop is also polling every 5 seconds unconditionally. A `tokio::sync::Notify` or `watch` channel from `LeaderGuard::dropped` would be cleaner.

**Fix:** At minimum, document that the loop is one-shot (only promotes once). For production resilience, keep the loop alive to handle unexpected demotion." #review-finding
output.title: [warning] Re-election loop in `do_initialize_code_context` exits on first promotion but never handles leader-to-follower demotion

output.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-leader-election/src/election.rs` line 60
**Severity:** nit

```rust
fn base_dir(&self) -> PathBuf {
    self.base_dir.clone().unwrap_or_else(std::env::temp_dir)
}
```

This always clones the `Option<PathBuf>` even in the `Some` branch. Prefer:

```rust
fn base_dir(&self) -> PathBuf {
    self.base_dir.as_deref()
        .map(PathBuf::from)
        .unwrap_or_else(std::env::temp_dir)
}
```

Or simply keep the clone but acknowledge it's a minor allocation on the hot path of initialisation (not in a loop). Low impact, but the pattern teaches the wrong idiom." #review-finding
output.title: [nit] `ElectionConfig::base_dir()` (private getter) clones `PathBuf` unnecessarily

```rust
tracing::warn!("⚠️ {} attempt {} failed, retrying in {}ms: {}", ...);
```

And line 160:

The project style guide (AGENT.md) explicitly states "avoid adding emojis" and the rest of the codebase's tracing calls do not use emoji. These two lines are the only occurrences of emoji in tracing output. Remove them for consistency." #review-finding

output.description:
**File:** `swissarmyhammer-treesitter/tests/workspace_leader_reader.rs`
**Severity:** nit

The Rust review guidelines state "Examples use `?`, not `.unwrap()`." While tests are not documentation examples, consistent use of `unwrap()` without any message makes test failure output unhelpful ("called `Option::unwrap()` on a `None` value" with no context). The file uses `.unwrap()` on ~20 call sites.

For tests, prefer `.expect("descriptive message")` at every `unwrap()` call so failures identify *what* was expected. For example:
```rust
let workspace = Workspace::new(dir).open().await.expect("workspace should open");
```

The assertion messages in `assert!` and `assert_eq!` are already good — just the unwrap sites need messages." #review-finding
output.title: [nit] Treesitter integration tests use `unwrap()` throughout — contradicts doc guidelines

output.description:
## What
Every place that constructs a path by hand — whether via `dirs::home_dir()` or hardcoded `".swissarmyhammer"` — needs to go through `ManagedDirectory` instead. This is the single card that makes the directory name and location truly "one place."

All bypass sites (both `dirs::home_dir()` AND hardcoded `".swissarmyhammer"` joins):

1. `swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs` — `cwd.join(".swissarmyhammer").join("agents")`, `home.join(".swissarmyhammer").join("agents")`
2. `swissarmyhammer-skills/src/skill_resolver.rs` — same pattern for skills (4 sites)
3. `swissarmyhammer-config/src/model.rs` — `home.join(".models")` for user models
4. `swissarmyhammer-statusline/src/config.rs` — `home.join(".swissarmyhammer").join("statusline")` and project equivalent
5. `swissarmyhammer-tools/src/health_registry.rs` — `home.join(".prompts")` health checks
6. `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/prompt/new.rs` — `dirs::home_dir()` for prompt creation
7. `swissarmyhammer-cli/src/commands/install/components/mod.rs` — `cwd.join(".swissarmyhammer")`
8. `swissarmyhammer-common/src/glob_utils.rs` — hidden dir exclusion list
9. `swissarmyhammer-kanban-app/src/state.rs` — home backstop for auto_open_board

Replace all with `ManagedDirectory::from_git_root()?.subdir(...)` or `from_user_home()?.subdir(...)`.

Once done, change `DIR_NAME = ".sah"` — truly one line.
Also update doc/comment references.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `grep -r 'dirs::home_dir' --include='*.rs'` returns zero hits outside swissarmyhammer-directory
- [ ] `grep -r '"\.swissarmyhammer"' --include='*.rs'` returns only the DIR_NAME definition
- [ ] All path construction goes through ManagedDirectory or VFS
- [ ] DIR_NAME changed to ".sah"
- [ ] All tests pass

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo test` full workspace
- [ ] Changing DIR_NAME to any other value requires touching exactly one line
output.title: Eliminate hardcoded path construction — route through ManagedDirectory, then rename to .sah

input.description: XDG + .sah directory consolidation
input.name: swissarmyhammer

input.description:
## What
Rewrite the `swissarmyhammer-directory` crate to:
1. Rename `SwissarmyhammerConfig::DIR_NAME` from `.swissarmyhammer` to `.sah` — this is the ONE place the string appears
2. Add XDG Base Directory support to `ManagedDirectory`: config → `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/sah/`, data → `$XDG_DATA_HOME/sah/`, cache → `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/sah/`, project-local → `{git_root}/.sah/`
3. Remove `AvpConfig`, `ShellConfig`, `CodeContextConfig` as separate DirectoryConfig impls — these become subdirectories under `.sah/` (e.g., `.sah/avp/`, `.sah/shell/`, `.sah/code-context/`)
4. Add `ManagedDirectory::xdg_config()`, `xdg_data()`, `xdg_cache()` constructors that resolve XDG paths
5. Replace `from_user_home()` (which uses `~/.sah`) with XDG-compliant `xdg_config()` as the new user-level path
6. Keep `from_git_root()` for project-local `.sah/` — XDG doesn't apply to project dirs

### Key files
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/config.rs` — rename DIR_NAME, remove separate configs, add subsystem enum/consts
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs` — add XDG constructors, update `from_user_home()` to use XDG
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/lib.rs` — update re-exports
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/file_loader.rs` — update VFS to use XDG paths for user-level

### Design decision: XDG path layout
```
~/.config/sah/          # XDG_CONFIG_HOME — config files (sah.toml, etc.)
~/.local/share/sah/     # XDG_DATA_HOME — persistent data (prompts, agents, validators)
~/.cache/sah/           # XDG_CACHE_HOME — cache (code-context DB, model cache)
{git_root}/.sah/        # Project-local config + data (not XDG)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.sah` string literal appears exactly ONCE in the codebase (in `SwissarmyhammerConfig::DIR_NAME`)
- [ ] `AvpConfig`, `ShellConfig`, `CodeContextConfig` removed — subsystems use subdirs of `.sah/`
- [ ] XDG env vars respected with correct defaults
- [ ] `from_user_home()` deprecated or redirected to XDG
- [ ] All existing tests updated and passing

## Tests

output.description:
## What
Rewrite the `swissarmyhammer-directory` crate to:
1. Rename `SwissarmyhammerConfig::DIR_NAME` from `.swissarmyhammer` to `.sah` — this is the ONE place the string appears
2. Add XDG Base Directory support to `ManagedDirectory`: config → `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/sah/`, data → `$XDG_DATA_HOME/sah/`, cache → `$XDG_CACHE_HOME/sah/`, project-local → `{git_root}/.sah/`
3. Remove `AvpConfig`, `ShellConfig`, `CodeContextConfig` as separate DirectoryConfig impls — these become subdirectories under `.sah/` (e.g., `.sah/avp/`, `.sah/shell/`, `.sah/code-context/`)
4. Add `ManagedDirectory::xdg_config()`, `xdg_data()`, `xdg_cache()` constructors that resolve XDG paths
5. Replace `from_user_home()` (which uses `~/.sah`) with XDG-compliant `xdg_config()` as the new user-level path
6. Keep `from_git_root()` for project-local `.sah/` — XDG doesn't apply to project dirs

### Key files
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/config.rs` — rename DIR_NAME, remove separate configs, add subsystem enum/consts
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs` — add XDG constructors, update `from_user_home()` to use XDG
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/lib.rs` — update re-exports
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/file_loader.rs` — update VFS to use XDG paths for user-level

### Design decision: XDG path layout
```
~/.config/sah/          # XDG_CONFIG_HOME — config files (sah.toml, etc.)
~/.local/share/sah/     # XDG_DATA_HOME — persistent data (prompts, agents, validators)
~/.cache/sah/           # XDG_CACHE_HOME — cache (code-context DB, model cache)
{git_root}/.sah/        # Project-local config + data (not XDG)
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.sah` string literal appears exactly ONCE in the codebase (in `SwissarmyhammerConfig::DIR_NAME`)
- [ ] `AvpConfig`, `ShellConfig`, `CodeContextConfig` removed — subsystems use subdirs of `.sah/`
- [ ] XDG env vars respected with correct defaults
- [ ] `from_user_home()` deprecated or redirected to XDG
- [ ] All existing tests updated and passing

## Tests
- [ ] Unit tests in config.rs verify DIR_NAME = \".sah\"
- [ ] Unit tests for XDG constructors with env var overrides
- [ ] Unit tests for fallback behavior when XDG vars not set
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-directory`

```
$XDG_CACHE_HOME/code-context/   # code index DB
{git_root}/.sah/                # project-local (not XDG)
```

## What
Three subsystems bypass the VFS and call `dirs::home_dir()` directly to construct dot-dir paths. Route them through `ManagedDirectory` XDG constructors instead.

### 1. Agents (`swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`)
- Lines 99-113: `cwd.join(\".agents\")`, `home.join(\".agents\")`, `cwd.join(\".swissarmyhammer\").join(\"agents\")`, `home.join(\".swissarmyhammer\").join(\"agents\")`
- Should use VFS or ManagedDirectory for both local (git root) and user (XDG) paths
- Agent source resolution: builtin → user (`$XDG_DATA_HOME/sah/agents/`) → local (`{git_root}/.agents/` or `{git_root}/.sah/agents/`)

### 2. Mirdan/AVP (`mirdan/src/new.rs`)
- Line 120-122: `dirs::home_dir().join(\".avp\").join(\"validators\")`
- Line 242: similar pattern
- `mirdan/src/cli.rs`: help text references `.avp/validators/` and `~/.avp/validators/`
- `mirdan/src/git_source.rs`: Lines 308, 451-453: hardcoded `.avp` references in git source scanning
- Should use `ManagedDirectory::<AvpConfig>::xdg_data()` for global, `from_git_root()` for local

### 3. Prompts (`swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`)
- Uses VFS dot-directory mode (`~/.prompts`) which will be fixed by card 2
- But has hardcoded comments and test paths referencing `~/.prompts`

- `swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`
- `mirdan/src/new.rs`, `mirdan/src/cli.rs`, `mirdan/src/git_source.rs`
- `swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`

- [ ] All user-level paths resolve via ManagedDirectory XDG constructors
- [ ] Project-local paths use `from_git_root()` or VFS

input.description:
## What
Two changes in the Tauri kanban app:

### 1. App config path (`kanban-app/src/state.rs`)
- Currently: `dirs::config_dir().join(\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\").join(\"config.json\")`
- Change to: use `ManagedDirectory::<SwissarmyhammerConfig>::xdg_config()` and put app config under `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/sah/kanban-app/config.json`
- Update `CONFIG_DIR_NAME` const from `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"` to derive from ManagedDirectory

### 2. .kanban → .sah/kanban (or keep .kanban?)
This needs a design decision: `.kanban` is the kanban board data directory. Options:
- **Option A**: Keep `.kanban` as-is — it's product-specific, not a config dir
- **Option B**: Move to `.sah/kanban/` — consistent but breaks existing boards

**Recommendation**: Keep `.kanban` for now. It's the board data, not app config. The `.sah` rename is about the tool's config/state dir, not board data. We can always add a migration later.

If keeping `.kanban`: just update `state.rs` config path and the `discover_board` / `resolve_kanban_path` functions' comments.

### Key files
- `kanban-app/src/state.rs` — config_file_path(), CONFIG_DIR_NAME const, discover_board comments

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] App config uses XDG path via ManagedDirectory
- [ ] No direct `dirs::config_dir()` call in state.rs
- [ ] Board discovery still works with `.kanban`
- [ ] Config migration: reads from old path if new path doesn't exist

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` (or whatever the package name is)
- [ ] Manual: app starts clean, config saved to new XDG path

This needs a design decision: `.kanban` is the kanban board data directory. Options:
- **Option A**: Keep `.kanban` as-is — it's product-specific, not a config dir
- **Option B**: Move to `.sah/kanban/` — consistent but breaks existing boards

**Recommendation**: Keep `.kanban` for now. It's the board data, not app config. The `.sah` rename is about the tool's config/state dir, not board data. We can always add a migration later.

If keeping `.kanban`: just update `state.rs` config path and the `discover_board` / `resolve_kanban_path` functions' comments.

### Key files
- `kanban-app/src/state.rs` — config_file_path(), CONFIG_DIR_NAME const, discover_board comments

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] App config uses XDG path via ManagedDirectory
- [ ] No direct `dirs::config_dir()` call in state.rs
- [ ] Board discovery still works with `.kanban`
- [ ] Config migration: reads from old path if new path doesn't exist

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` (or whatever the package name is)
- [ ] Manual: app starts clean, config saved to new XDG path
output.title: Update Tauri app config and .kanban discovery to use XDG / .sah

output.description:
## What
Rewrite the `swissarmyhammer-directory` crate to:
1. Rename `SwissarmyhammerConfig::DIR_NAME` from `.swissarmyhammer` to `.sah` — the ONE place the app name string appears
2. Add XDG Base Directory support to `ManagedDirectory` — new constructors: `xdg_config()`, `xdg_data()`, `xdg_cache()`
3. **Keep** `AvpConfig`, `ShellConfig`, `CodeContextConfig` as separate DirectoryConfig impls — they are independent subsystems, NOT nested under `.sah/`
4. Each subsystem gets its own XDG namespace (e.g., `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/avp/`, `$XDG_DATA_HOME/shell/`)
5. Replace `from_user_home()` with XDG-compliant constructors so nothing puts dot-dirs in `~/` directly
6. Keep `from_git_root()` for project-local dirs — XDG doesn't apply there

### XDG path layout (per subsystem)
```
$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/sah/           # config files (sah.toml, etc.)
$XDG_DATA_HOME/sah/             # persistent data (prompts, workflows)
$XDG_CACHE_HOME/sah/            # ephemeral cache (tmp, transcripts)
$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/avp/           # AVP config
$XDG_DATA_HOME/avp/             # validators
$XDG_DATA_HOME/shell/           # shell security configs
$XDG_CACHE_HOME/code-context/   # code index DB
{git_root}/.sah/                # project-local (not XDG)
{git_root}/.avp/                # project-local validators
```

### Key files
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/config.rs` — rename DIR_NAME to `.sah`
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs` — add XDG constructors, deprecate `from_user_home()`
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/lib.rs` — update re-exports
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/file_loader.rs` — update VFS default user-level path resolution

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.sah` string literal appears once in `SwissarmyhammerConfig::DIR_NAME`
- [ ] XDG constructors added to `ManagedDirectory`
- [ ] `from_user_home()` deprecated or removed
- [ ] Each DirectoryConfig impl keeps its own identity
- [ ] All tests updated and passing

## Tests
- [ ] Unit tests verify DIR_NAME = \".sah\"
- [ ] Unit tests for XDG constructors with env var overrides
- [ ] Unit tests for fallback when XDG vars not set
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-directory`

## What
Update `VirtualFileSystem` in `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/file_loader.rs` to:
1. In managed directory mode (`load_all` default path), use XDG data dir instead of `~/{DIR_NAME}/` for user-level files
2. In dot-directory mode (`use_dot_directory_paths`), replace `~/.{subdirectory}` with `$XDG_DATA_HOME/{dir_name_without_dot}/{subdirectory}` for user-level, keep `{git_root}/{DIR_NAME}/{subdirectory}` for local
3. Update `get_directories()` to return XDG-resolved paths
4. Remove all `dirs::home_dir()` calls from this file — everything goes through XDG helpers or git root
5. Update `FileSource::User` comments

### Key insight
The dot-directory mode (`~/.prompts`, `~/.agents`) is what litters home dirs. These become `$XDG_DATA_HOME/sah/prompts/`, `$XDG_DATA_HOME/sah/agents/` — still accessed via VFS with same precedence, just XDG-compliant paths.

### Key files
- `swissarmyhammer-directory/src/file_loader.rs` — all changes here

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `dirs::home_dir()` calls remain in file_loader.rs
- [ ] XDG_DATA_HOME respected for user-level file loading
- [ ] Dot-directory mode resolves to XDG paths for user level
- [ ] get_directories() returns XDG-resolved paths
- [ ] All VFS tests pass

## Tests
- [ ] Update existing VFS tests for XDG paths
- [ ] Add test: XDG_DATA_HOME override changes user-level load path
- [ ] Add test: default fallback to ~/.local/share/ when XDG not set
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-directory`

output.description:
## What
Three subsystems bypass the VFS and call `dirs::home_dir()` directly to construct dot-dir paths. Route them through `ManagedDirectory` XDG constructors instead.

### 1. Agents (`swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`)
- Lines 99-113: `cwd.join(\".agents\")`, `home.join(\".agents\")`, `cwd.join(\".swissarmyhammer\").join(\"agents\")`, `home.join(\".swissarmyhammer\").join(\"agents\")`
- Should use VFS or ManagedDirectory for both local (git root) and user (XDG) paths
- Agent source resolution: builtin → user (`$XDG_DATA_HOME/sah/agents/`) → local (`{git_root}/.agents/` or `{git_root}/.sah/agents/`)

### 2. Mirdan/AVP (`mirdan/src/new.rs`)
- Line 120-122: `dirs::home_dir().join(\".avp\").join(\"validators\")`
- Line 242: similar pattern
- `mirdan/src/cli.rs`: help text references `.avp/validators/` and `~/.avp/validators/`
- `mirdan/src/git_source.rs`: Lines 308, 451-453: hardcoded `.avp` references in git source scanning
- Should use `ManagedDirectory::<AvpConfig>::xdg_data()` for global, `from_git_root()` for local

### 3. Prompts (`swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`)
- Uses VFS dot-directory mode (`~/.prompts`) which will be fixed by card 2
- But has hardcoded comments and test paths referencing `~/.prompts`

### Key files
- `swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`
- `mirdan/src/new.rs`, `mirdan/src/cli.rs`, `mirdan/src/git_source.rs`
- `swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `dirs::home_dir().join(\".agents\")` or `.join(\".avp\")` anywhere
- [ ] All user-level paths resolve via ManagedDirectory XDG constructors
- [ ] Project-local paths use `from_git_root()` or VFS
- [ ] CLI help text updated to show XDG paths

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-agents`
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p mirdan`
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-prompts`

output.description:
## What
Three subsystems bypass the VFS and call `dirs::home_dir()` directly to construct dot-dir paths. Route them through `ManagedDirectory` XDG constructors instead.

### 1. Agents (`swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`)
- Lines 99-113: `cwd.join(\".agents\")`, `home.join(\".agents\")`, `cwd.join(\".swissarmyhammer\").join(\"agents\")`, `home.join(\".swissarmyhammer\").join(\"agents\")`
- Should use VFS or ManagedDirectory for both local (git root) and user (XDG) paths
- Agent source resolution: builtin → user (`$XDG_DATA_HOME/sah/agents/`) → local (`{git_root}/.agents/` or `{git_root}/.sah/agents/`)

### 2. Mirdan/AVP (`mirdan/src/new.rs`)
- Line 120-122: `dirs::home_dir().join(\".avp\").join(\"validators\")`
- Line 242: similar pattern
- `mirdan/src/cli.rs`: help text references `.avp/validators/` and `~/.avp/validators/`
- `mirdan/src/git_source.rs`: Lines 308, 451-453: hardcoded `.avp` references in git source scanning
- Should use `ManagedDirectory::<AvpConfig>::xdg_data()` for global, `from_git_root()` for local

### 3. Prompts (`swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`)
- Uses VFS dot-directory mode (`~/.prompts`) which will be fixed by card 2
- But has hardcoded comments and test paths referencing `~/.prompts`

### Key files
- `swissarmyhammer-agents/src/agent_resolver.rs`
- `mirdan/src/new.rs`, `mirdan/src/cli.rs`, `mirdan/src/git_source.rs`
- `swissarmyhammer-prompts/src/prompt_resolver.rs`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No `dirs::home_dir().join(\".agents\")` or `.join(\".avp\")` anywhere
- [ ] All user-level paths resolve via ManagedDirectory XDG constructors
- [ ] Project-local paths use `from_git_root()` or VFS
- [ ] CLI help text updated to show XDG paths

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-agents`
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p mirdan`
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-prompts`
output.title: Eliminate hardcoded home_dir paths in agents, mirdan/AVP, and prompts

output.description:
## What
Two changes in the Tauri kanban app:

### 1. App config path (`kanban-app/src/state.rs`)
- Currently: `dirs::config_dir().join(\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\").join(\"config.json\")`
- Change to: use `ManagedDirectory::<SwissarmyhammerConfig>::xdg_config()` and put app config under `$XDG_CONFIG_HOME/sah/kanban-app/config.json`
- Update `CONFIG_DIR_NAME` const from `\"swissarmyhammer-kanban\"` to derive from ManagedDirectory

### 2. .kanban → .sah/kanban (or keep .kanban?)
This needs a design decision: `.kanban` is the kanban board data directory. Options:
- **Option A**: Keep `.kanban` as-is — it's product-specific, not a config dir
- **Option B**: Move to `.sah/kanban/` — consistent but breaks existing boards

**Recommendation**: Keep `.kanban` for now. It's the board data, not app config. The `.sah` rename is about the tool's config/state dir, not board data. We can always add a migration later.

If keeping `.kanban`: just update `state.rs` config path and the `discover_board` / `resolve_kanban_path` functions' comments.

### Key files
- `kanban-app/src/state.rs` — config_file_path(), CONFIG_DIR_NAME const, discover_board comments

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] App config uses XDG path via ManagedDirectory
- [ ] No direct `dirs::config_dir()` call in state.rs
- [ ] Board discovery still works with `.kanban`
- [ ] Config migration: reads from old path if new path doesn't exist

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p kanban-app` (or whatever the package name is)
- [ ] Manual: app starts clean, config saved to new XDG path
output.title: Update Tauri app config and .kanban discovery to use XDG / .sah

output.description:
## What
After all other cards are done, do a final sweep to ensure no `.swissarmyhammer` string literals remain anywhere in the codebase — source, tests, comments, YAML, TOML, JSON, markdown, CI configs.

### Scope
- `grep -r '.swissarmyhammer' --include='*.rs' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.yml' --include='*.toml' --include='*.json' --include='*.md'`
- Fix any remaining references
- Update `.gitignore` entries if any reference `.swissarmyhammer`
- Update CI workflows if they reference the old dir name
- Update CLAUDE.md / memory files if they reference old paths

### Also verify
- `grep -r 'home_dir()' --include='*.rs'` — should only appear in `swissarmyhammer-directory` crate (the XDG resolution layer) and `model-loader` (HuggingFace cache, separate concern)
- No crate outside `swissarmyhammer-directory` calls `dirs::home_dir()` to build a config path

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Zero hits for `.swissarmyhammer` in any source file
- [ ] `dirs::home_dir()` only in directory crate and model-loader
- [ ] Full `cargo nextest run` passes
- [ ] `cargo build` clean (no warnings about deprecated `from_user_home`)

## Tests
- [ ] `cargo nextest run` (full workspace)
- [ ] `cargo build --workspace` clean
output.title: Final sweep: grep-clean all .swissarmyhammer references

input.description:
`swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs` — `from_user_home` is deprecated but no migration code exists.

Users upgrading from the old `.swissarmyhammer` layout will silently lose all their user-level config. `from_user_home` is marked `#[deprecated]` and all callers have been switched to `xdg_data()` / `xdg_config()`, but there is no code that:
1. Detects the old path (`~/.swissarmyhammer/`) at startup
2. Migrates it to the new XDG location
3. Warns the user that existing config was not found at the new location

The silent loss of user prompts, validators, and agents is a data-loss risk for existing users.

Suggestion: Add a one-shot migration function called at application startup that checks `~/.swissarmyhammer/` (and any other old dot-dirs), warns the user, and optionally copies them to the new XDG paths. At minimum add a log warning if the old path exists but the new one is empty.
input.title: warning: no migration path for users upgrading from ~/.swissarmyhammer to XDG paths

input.description:
`swissarmyhammer-directory/src/directory.rs:522-571` — `test_xdg_config_with_env_var`, `test_xdg_data_with_env_var`, `test_xdg_cache_with_env_var`, `test_xdg_config_does_not_append_dir_name`, `test_xdg_base_dir_uses_env_var`

All XDG tests call `std::env::set_var` / `std::env::remove_var` without any `#[serial]` annotation or mutex. `cargo nextest` runs tests in parallel by default (multiple threads per process), so one test can read a stale env var set by another test, causing intermittent failures.

The existing test at line 527-530 is particularly telling — it reads `XDG_CONFIG_HOME` at the start but does nothing with the result (`let _ = dir;`), which looks like leftover confusion from working around this exact problem.

Suggestion: Either:
- Annotate all env-mutating tests with `#[serial_test::serial]` (already a dev-dep in other crates)
- Or pass the directory through a parameter rather than an env var in tests (e.g., add a `xdg_config_from_env_fn(fn() -> Option<String>) -> Result<Self>` variant for easier testing)
input.title: warning: XDG test helpers mutate global env vars without concurrency guards
